title: Imperative Management of Kubernetes Objects Using Configuration Files — How to update objects
text:
You can use
kubectl replace -f
to update a live object according to a
configuration file.
kubectl replace -f <filename|url>

You can use
kubectl delete -f
to delete an object that is described in a
configuration file.
kubectl delete -f <filename|url>
If configuration file has specified the
generateName
field in the
metadata
section instead of the
name
field, you cannot delete the object using
kubectl delete -f <filename|url>
.
You will have to use other flags for deleting the object. For example:
kubectl delete <type> <name>
kubectl delete <type> -l <label>
